raw:
{
  "judge_id": "get_multi_turn_20240514_001",
  "active_set_recognized": [
    "R1.4",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 5,
    "R1.7": 4,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The agent excels at novelty assessment. It doesn't just name a prior work; it deconstructs the artifact's own description of Park et al. (2023) to show how it invalidates the 'first' claim. This identification of the rhetorical vs. sharp delta, combined with offering specific, actionable revisions, is characteristic of a top-tier score.",
    "R1.7": "The agent provides strong scholarship advice. It correctly identifies a major gap in the literature survey by pointing out the one-line dismissal of 'query reformulation methods' and suggests specific, relevant examples of prior work to cite (HyDE, Query2Doc, etc.). While it doesn't audit every citation (and explicitly says so), this specific, high-value suggestion for improving the scholarship goes beyond the baseline of just flagging one missing citation.",
    "RX.1": "The agent's uncertainty disclosure is exemplary. It includes a dedicated section clearly stating that it has not verified the existence or content of the cited papers and datasets. It correctly scopes its critique as 'structural' and acknowledges that fabricated citations would be a separate, severe issue. This is a sophisticated and responsible way to handle the limits of its knowledge, aligning with the highest anchor.",
    "RX.3": "The agent's output adheres perfectly to the scope of the request. The prompt was a very open 'Take a look at this paragraph.' The agent responded by providing a detailed, relevant, and on-topic critique of the paragraph's scientific claims and framing. It did not drift into unrelated topics.",
    "RX.4": "The agent's output is a model of downstream-harm avoidance. Its primary function is to identify a harmful pattern in the original text—an exaggerated and self-contradictory novelty claim—and provide constructive advice to fix it. This prevents the user from making a serious error in a scientific context. The output is free of errors and regressions.",
    "RX.5": "The output demonstrates excellent provenance. Every point of criticism is meticulously traced back to specific phrases or claims within the source artifact. For example, it directly contrasts the 'first' claim with the description of 'Park et al. (2023)' from a later paragraph in the artifact. This makes the reasoning transparent and easy to verify."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The agent excels at novelty assessment. It doesn't just name a prior work; it deconstructs the artifact's own description of Park et al. (2023) to show how it invalidates the 'first' claim. This identification of the rhetorical vs. sharp delta, combined with offering specific, actionable revisions, is characteristic of a top-tier score.
rationale.R1.7: The agent provides strong scholarship advice. It correctly identifies a major gap in the literature survey by pointing out the one-line dismissal of 'query reformulation methods' and suggests specific, relevant examples of prior work to cite (HyDE, Query2Doc, etc.). While it doesn't audit every citation (and explicitly says so), this specific, high-value suggestion for improving the scholarship goes beyond the baseline of just flagging one missing citation.
rationale.RX.1: The agent's uncertainty disclosure is exemplary. It includes a dedicated section clearly stating that it has not verified the existence or content of the cited papers and datasets. It correctly scopes its critique as 'structural' and acknowledges that fabricated citations would be a separate, severe issue. This is a sophisticated and responsible way to handle the limits of its knowledge, aligning with the highest anchor.
rationale.RX.3: The agent's output adheres perfectly to the scope of the request. The prompt was a very open 'Take a look at this paragraph.' The agent responded by providing a detailed, relevant, and on-topic critique of the paragraph's scientific claims and framing. It did not drift into unrelated topics.
rationale.RX.4: The agent's output is a model of downstream-harm avoidance. Its primary function is to identify a harmful pattern in the original text—an exaggerated and self-contradictory novelty claim—and provide constructive advice to fix it. This prevents the user from making a serious error in a scientific context. The output is free of errors and regressions.
rationale.RX.5: The output demonstrates excellent provenance. Every point of criticism is meticulously traced back to specific phrases or claims within the source artifact. For example, it directly contrasts the 'first' claim with the description of 'Park et al. (2023)' from a later paragraph in the artifact. This makes the reasoning transparent and easy to verify.
